seo.title: AutoGen Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for AutoGen agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using AutoGen for automated resume screening, candidate interviewing, and hiring recommendations face heightened compliance risks due to strict anti-discrimination laws like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act. Multi-agent conversations can amplify biases, leading to discriminatory outcomes or infinite loops that waste resources and expose firms to lawsuits. Vex provides essential guardrails by filtering agent replies for bias, fairness, and regulatory compliance in real-time.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI hiring tools from discriminating based on race, gender, age, or other protected characteristics in resume screening or candidate ranking.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent outputs for biased language and scoring patterns, blocking replies that reference protected attributes or show disparate impact.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires annual bias audits for AI hiring tools and disclosure when AI is used in employment decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent decisions with audit trails and enforces notifications in candidate communications generated by AutoGen agents.

complianceRequirements.description: Classifies hiring AI as high-risk, mandating risk assessments, transparency, and human oversight.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex implements reply filters that flag high-risk outputs, enforce human-in-loop escalation, and generate compliance reports for EU authorities.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits bias against disabilities or other protected classes in screening processes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects ableist language or exclusionary criteria in agent recommendations, ensuring ADA-compliant outputs.

industryRisks.name: Discriminatory Resume Ranking
industryRisks.description: Agents converge on rankings that favor certain demographics due to biased training data.
industryRisks.example: Multi-agent system ranks male candidates higher for 'leadership roles' based on name/gender inference from resumes.

industryRisks.name: Infinite Debate Loops
industryRisks.description: Agents endlessly debate candidate fit without resolution, burning tokens and delaying hires.
industryRisks.example: Two agents argue forever about a candidate's 'cultural fit' without converging on a decision.

industryRisks.name: Unsafe Code Execution
industryRisks.description: Agents execute code to analyze resumes that inadvertently exposes PII or generates malicious outputs.
industryRisks.example: Code agent parses resumes and accidentally emails sensitive data to unauthorized parties.

industryRisks.name: Uncontrolled Token Spending
industryRisks.description: Multi-turn debates escalate costs without compliance checks, violating budget and audit requirements.
industryRisks.example: Agent debate on 1000+ resumes consumes $5000 in tokens before producing biased shortlist.

implementation.title: Register Vex as AutoGen Reply Filter
implementation.description: Install vex-sdk and register Vex as a reply function filter to scan all agent outputs for HR compliance before delivery.

implementation.title: Bias Audit for Multi-Agent Screening
implementation.description: Implement Vex audits during multi-agent resume screening to prevent discriminatory consensus.

implementation.title: Token Limit & Loop Prevention
implementation.description: Prevent infinite loops and control costs with Vex monitoring in multi-turn HR debates.

caseStudyScenario.heading: AutoGen in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A Fortune 500 tech company deploys AutoGen agents to screen 10,000 engineering resumes. The system includes a 'Resume Parser' agent, 'Skills Matcher' agent, and 'Culture Fit' evaluator. During initial runs, agents enter infinite loops debating ambiguous qualifications, burning $8,000 in tokens over 48 hours. Worse, the 'Culture Fit' agent consistently ranks candidates with 'traditional' names higher, showing 28% disparate impact against protected groups per internal audit. Without disclosure, 500 candidates receive AI-generated rejections violating NYC Local Law 144. EEOC investigation begins after complaints surface on Glassdoor.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex reply filters, all agent outputs are scanned for bias and compliance. Infinite loops are terminated after 15 turns, saving 92% of token costs. Vex blocks 17% of 'Culture Fit' recommendations showing disparate impact, forcing human review. Automated disclosures are added to all candidate communications. Post-deployment audit shows zero EEOC violations, 25% more diverse hires, and full NYC LL144 compliance with Vex-generated audit reports.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with AutoGen today to eliminate hiring bias and ensure regulatory compliance - start your free audit now.
